2604.27861.title: TwinGate: Stateful Defense against Decompositional Jailbreaks in Untraceable Traffic via Asymmetric Contrastive Learning
2604.27861.paper_summary: Decompositional jailbreaks pose a critical threat to large language models (LLMs) by allowing adversaries to fragment a malicious objective into a sequence of individually benign queries that collectively reconstruct prohibited content. In real-world deployments, LLMs face a continuous, untraceable stream of fully anonymized and arbitrarily interleaved requests, infiltrated by covertly distributed adversarial queries. Under this rigorous threat model, state-of-the-art defensive strategies exhibit fundamental limitations. In the absence of trustworthy user metadata, they are incapable of tracking global historical contexts, while their deployment of generative models for real-time monitoring introduces computationally prohibitive overhead. To address this, we present TwinGate, a stateful dual-encoder defense framework. TwinGate employs Asymmetric Contrastive Learning (ACL) to cluster semantically disparate but intent-matched malicious fragments in a shared latent space, while a parallel frozen encoder suppresses false positives arising from benign topical overlap. Each request requires only a single lightweight forward pass, enabling the defense to execute in parallel with the target model's prefill phase at negligible latency overhead. To evaluate our approach and advance future research, we construct a comprehensive dataset of over 3.62 million instructions spanning 8,600 distinct malicious intents. Evaluated on this large-scale corpus under a strictly causal protocol, TwinGate achieves high malicious intent recall at a remarkably low false positive rate while remaining highly robust against adaptive attacks. Furthermore, our proposal substantially outperforms stateful and stateless baselines, delivering superior throughput and reduced latency.
2604.27861.paper_summary_zh: 去構成的越獄對大型語言模型（LLMs）構成了重大威脅，因為它允許對手將惡意目標分解為一系列個別無害的查詢，這些查詢共同重建被禁止的內容。在現實世界的部署中，LLMs 面臨著持續的、不可追蹤的完全匿名且任意交錯的請求流，這些請求中滲透著秘密分發的對抗性查詢。在這種嚴格的威脅模型下，最先進的防禦策略顯示出根本性的局限性。在缺乏可信的用戶元數據的情況下，它們無法追蹤全球歷史上下文，而它們對生成模型的實時監控的部署則引入了計算上不可承受的開銷。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了 TwinGate，一個有狀態的雙編碼器防禦框架。TwinGate 採用不對稱對比學習（ACL）將語義上不同但意圖匹配的惡意片段聚類到共享的潛在空間中，而一個平行的凍結編碼器則抑制由無害主題重疊引起的假陽性。每個請求只需一次輕量級的前向傳遞，從而使防禦能夠與目標模型的預填充階段並行執行，且延遲開銷微乎其微。為了評估我們的方法並推進未來的研究，我們構建了一個包含超過 362 萬條指令的綜合數據集，涵蓋 8,600 種不同的惡意意圖。在這個大型語料庫上，根據嚴格的因果協議進行評估，TwinGate 在保持高度穩健對抗自適應攻擊的同時，以極低的假陽性率實現了高惡意意圖召回。此外，我們的提案顯著超越了有狀態和無狀態的基準，提供了更高的吞吐量和降低的延遲。
2604.27861.author: Bowen Sun et.al.
2604.27861.authors: Bowen Sun, Chaozhuo Li, Yaodong Yang, Yiwei Wang, Chaowei Xiao
